question: Will the highest temperature in Toronto be -2°C on March 23?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 23 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ca/mississauga/CYYZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Environment Canada and ECMWF model ensembles point to a daytime high near 2°C in Toronto on March 23, driven by a deep upper trough funneling Arctic air southward via northerly winds, underpinning the 34% trader consensus for 2°C over 3°C at 26.5%. Differentiating factors include potential cloud cover modulating insolation—favoring 3-4°C if stratus thickens—or clearer skies reinforcing sub-2°C outcomes amid radiative cooling. Historical March 23 highs average 5°C, but this anomalously cool pattern, with 850mb temps -10°C, caps upside; 1°C and below hold slim odds barring surprise reinforcement. Traders eye 00z GFS updates for front timing shifts impacting final probabilities.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment clusters tightly around 50-53°F highs for Seattle on March 23, driven by the latest GFS and ECMWF ensemble forecasts projecting peak afternoon temperatures of 50-52°F under partly cloudy skies with light southerly winds. These models account for persistent marine stratus delaying morning warmup, capping diurnal heating at Sea-Tac Airport's measurement site, where historical March 23 averages hover near 54°F but recent cool anomalies from Pacific low-pressure troughs pull odds downward. Differentiating factors include potential for brief sunbreaks boosting 52-53°F (33.5% implied probability) versus thicker cloud decks favoring 50-51°F (36%), with NOAA's high-resolution HRRR runs updating hourly through midday offering traders key resolution edges amid model spread of ±2°F.

Differentiating factors include potential for brief sunbreaks boosting 52-53°F (33.5% implied probability) versus thicker cloud decks favoring 50-51°F (36%), with NOAA's high-resolution HRRR runs updating hourly through midday offering traders key resolution edges amid model spread of ±2°F.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors a high of 51°F or below in New York City on March 23 at 53.5% implied probability, driven by the latest NOAA GFS and ECMWF model runs projecting daytime highs of 48-52°F amid a persistent cool upper-air pattern with northerly winds and lingering cloud cover from a recent frontal boundary. Historical data shows March 23 averages around 52°F at Central Park, but current 500-mb charts indicate a deep trough over the Northeast suppressing temperatures below seasonal norms. Yesterday's 12Z model updates reinforced this cool bias, with ensemble spreads of ±3°F centering outcomes in the 48-53°F range, while slim odds for warmer scenarios reflect low-confidence ridging signals. Key watch: tomorrow's 00Z forecasts could shift probabilities if boundary layer mixing strengthens.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the LaGuardia Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 23 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the LaGuardia Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ny/new-york-city/KLGA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors a high of 51°F or below in New York City on March 23 at 53.5% implied probability, driven by the latest NOAA GFS and ECMWF model runs projecting daytime highs of 48-52°F amid a persistent cool upper-air pattern with northerly winds and lingering cloud cover from a recent frontal boundary. Historical data shows March 23 averages around 52°F at Central Park, but current 500-mb charts indicate a deep trough over the Northeast suppressing temperatures below seasonal norms. Yesterday's 12Z model updates reinforced this cool bias, with ensemble spreads of ±3°F centering outcomes in the 48-53°F range, while slim odds for warmer scenarios reflect low-confidence ridging signals. Key watch: tomorrow's 00Z forecasts could shift probabilities if boundary layer mixing strengthens.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Dallas's March 23 high temperature tilts toward 78-81°F, with 78-79°F leading at 30% and 80-81°F close at 26%, propelled by National Weather Service forecasts pinpointing 79-82°F under a robust high-pressure ridge fostering southerly winds and above-normal warmth. Key differentiators include ensemble model divergences—GFS ensembles averaging ~81°F from stronger low-level jet moisture transport, versus ECMWF's cooler ~78°F bias due to lingering mid-level clouds—against Dallas's March climatology (mean high 68°F, but ridge patterns often exceed 80°F). Recent 00z runs show slight warming trends, though boundary-layer instability and diurnal heating caps introduce uncertainty, with 12z updates pivotal for resolution shifts per NOAA protocols.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Dallas's March 23 high temperature tilts toward 78-81°F, with 78-79°F leading at 30% and 80-81°F close at 26%, propelled by National Weather Service forecasts pinpointing 79-82°F under a robust high-pressure ridge fostering southerly winds and above-normal warmth. Key differentiators include ensemble model divergences—GFS ensembles averaging ~81°F from stronger low-level jet moisture transport, versus ECMWF's cooler ~78°F bias due to lingering mid-level clouds—against Dallas's March climatology (mean high 68°F, but ridge patterns often exceed 80°F). Recent 00z runs show slight warming trends, though boundary-layer instability and diurnal heating caps introduce uncertainty, with 12z updates pivotal for resolution shifts per NOAA protocols.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest National Weather Service forecasts for Atlanta project a March 23 high of 77-79°F under a strengthening upper-level ridge fostering warm southerly advection from the Gulf of Mexico, fueling trader consensus with 78-79°F (35.5%) edging 76-77°F (29.5%). Ensemble models like GFS and ECMWF exhibit minimal spread in this range, but subtle differences hinge on diurnal cloud development: earlier fair-weather cumulus onset could suppress peaks to 77°F via enhanced shading, while delayed clearing allows greater insolation for 79°F. This warm anomaly exceeds the 66°F March 23 climatological average, with low precipitation odds amplifying heating potential ahead of 12Z model updates.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chicago O'Hare Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/il/chicago/KORD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest NOAA Weather Prediction Center and National Weather Service forecasts for Chicago on March 23 point to a high temperature clustering around 41-43°F, driving trader sentiment toward the top two outcomes at 30.5% and 27.0% odds, reflecting ensemble model consensus from GFS and ECMWF runs updated overnight. Differentiating factors include subtle model divergences on boundary layer mixing and afternoon cloud cover—clearer skies could push toward 42-43°F via enhanced solar insolation, while increased low-level stratus favors 40-41°F stagnation. Historical March 23 norms average 43°F with a 2-3°F standard deviation, amplifying sensitivity to these micro-forecast tweaks amid neutral Arctic air advection; watch 12Z model updates for shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors 11°C (45%) and 12°C (34%) as Ankara's highest temperature on March 23, driven by ensemble weather model forecasts from ECMWF and GFS converging on mild spring conditions amid a high-pressure ridge over central Anatolia. Recent Turkish State Meteorological Service (MGM) outlooks confirm daytime highs near 11-12°C, following a pattern of moderating northerly winds after early March's cooler anomalies. Historical March data for Ankara shows average highs of 11.5°C, with low variability (±2°C) under similar synoptic setups, positioning 10°C (13.5%) as a conservative outlier. Minimal support for extremes reflects stable soil temperatures and no polar air intrusions in short-range projections.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Wellington Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 23 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Wellington Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/nz/wellington/NZWN.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: MetService New Zealand's latest forecast pins Wellington's March 23 high at 21°C under a stabilizing high-pressure ridge, driving trader consensus with 48.5% implied probability, while 22°C odds at 32% reflect minor model warm biases in GFS and ECMWF runs. Historical March maxima average 20.1°C at Kelburn station, with recent observations showing above-normal warmth this month amid La Niña influences suppressing extremes. Light northerlies and coastal sea breezes cap upside risks, positioning 20°C (14%) as a cool outlier from potential cloud incursions, as verified monitoring data underscores low volatility in late-summer ridge patterns. Upcoming 0600 NZST update could shift odds if southerlies strengthen.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors a high of 31°C or below in Lucknow on March 23 (87% implied probability), driven by India Meteorological Department (IMD) forecasts predicting a maximum around 30-31°C amid mild seasonal conditions and lingering winter influences. March norms for Lucknow typically see daytime highs of 28-32°C, with current upper-air patterns suppressing heat via moderate northeasterly winds and partial cloud cover from recent western disturbances. Short-range models like GFS and ECMWF align on subdued warming, with no heatwave signals; recent observations show daytime peaks at 29-30°C over the past week, reinforcing low odds for 32°C+ outliers (under 12% combined). Monitor IMD's evening update for final tweaks.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by NOAA at the Ben Gurion International Airport in degrees Celsius on 23 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from NOAA, specifically the highest reading under the "Temp" column on the specified date once information is finalized for all hours on that date, available here: https://www.weather.gov/wrh/timeseries?site=LLBG

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the "Switch to Metric Units" button until the relevant table displays °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ensemble weather models from the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) and GFS, corroborated by the Israel Meteorological Service, drive trader consensus toward a Tel Aviv high of 20°C (39%) or 21°C (23%) on March 23, aligning with late-winter Mediterranean norms of 19-22°C amid persistent high-pressure systems. Recent 00Z model runs show minimal spread, with 500hPa heights favoring mild advection and light southerlies limiting extremes, while sea surface temperatures around 18°C temper coastal warming. Historical data from Ben Gurion Airport records confirm similar March 23 averages near 20.5°C, with no anomalous signals like Saharan dust or cold outbreaks shifting probabilities; traders eye afternoon updates for final tweaks.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors a Tokyo high of 14°C on March 23, driven by Japan Meteorological Agency forecasts projecting mild conditions from persistent cool northerly flows and stable high-pressure systems over the region. Historical March averages hover around 13°C, with recent data showing Tokyo's early-spring highs in the 10-13°C range amid below-normal sea surface temperatures in the East China Sea limiting warm advection. Ensemble weather models like ECMWF and GFS converge on 13-15°C peaks, with minimal upside risk from frontal activity; a shift to warmer southerlies remains unlikely per upper-air analyses, keeping 15°C odds at 18% and higher temps negligible. Upcoming 12-hour updates could refine these market-implied probabilities.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's latest forecast pinning the March 23 maximum at 27°C drives the market's 49% implied probability for that outcome, amid a persistent high-pressure ridge funneling warm continental air into the region. Recent developments, including record-tying 28.5°C highs on March 21 and 28°C on March 22, bolster odds for 28°C or higher at 25.5%, while easterly winds may cap peaks, supporting 26°C (18.5%) and 25°C (11%). March norms hover around 24°C, but this anomalously warm spell—fueled by suppressed monsoon activity—elevates above-normal temperatures, with model ensembles converging on mid-to-upper 20s despite minor forecast divergences. Traders eye afternoon updates for shifts.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's latest forecast pinning the March 23 maximum at 27°C drives the market's 49% implied probability for that outcome, amid a persistent high-pressure ridge funneling warm continental air into the region. Recent developments, including record-tying 28.5°C highs on March 21 and 28°C on March 22, bolster odds for 28°C or higher at 25.5%, while easterly winds may cap peaks, supporting 26°C (18.5%) and 25°C (11%). March norms hover around 24°C, but this anomalously warm spell—fueled by suppressed monsoon activity—elevates above-normal temperatures, with model ensembles converging on mid-to-upper 20s despite minor forecast divergences. Traders eye afternoon updates for shifts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors a Shanghai high of 15°C (53%) on March 23, driven by the latest ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecasts converging on mild spring conditions with highs in the mid-teens Celsius amid weak cold air outbreaks from the north. Recent developments, including updated model runs from the China Meteorological Administration showing light southerly winds and partial cloud cover, have boosted probabilities for 14–16°C outcomes while diminishing outliers like 20°C+. Historical March 23 averages hover around 14°C, but urban heat island effects and current La Niña decay support this tempered warming; watch tomorrow's 12Z forecast update for potential shifts as resolution nears midnight UTC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest ECMWF and GFS ensemble forecasts pinpoint a high of 16°C as the most probable peak temperature in Milan on March 23, driving its 48.5% implied probability amid a high-pressure ridge ushering mild southerly flow after recent cool snaps. March climatology shows average highs near 14°C, but current upper-air patterns favor 15-17°C outcomes, with 17°C (24.5%) and 15°C (19%) close behind due to minor model divergences in boundary layer mixing and cloud cover. Updated 12Z runs yesterday slightly trimmed warmer outliers, compressing probabilities toward these leaders while low odds reflect rare extremes; traders eye afternoon data releases from Italian meteorological services for final tweaks.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus tilts toward a 17°C high in Madrid on March 23 (45% implied probability) over 18°C (38.5%), propelled by the latest ensemble forecasts from ECMWF and GFS models, which converge on 16.5–17.5°C amid a weak Atlantic ridge bringing mild southerly flow but persistent mid-level clouds. Recent 12Z model runs refined downward from prior 18°C biases due to enhanced stratocumulus cover reducing insolation, with numerical weather prediction uncertainty at ±1.5°C rooted in boundary layer mixing and urban heat island effects. Historical March highs average 16.8°C, but today's tighter 17–18°C race hinges on afternoon clearing; AEMET's official outlook supports the lower end absent stronger subsidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Consensus among leading ensemble weather models like ECMWF and GFS drives the 51.5% implied probability for Warsaw's highest temperature hitting 15°C on March 23, with recent runs converging on mild highs of 14-16°C amid a high-pressure ridge ushering Atlantic warmth into Central Europe. Official Polish IMGW forecasts align, projecting daytime peaks near 15°C under partly cloudy skies, up from cooler ensemble means a week ago due to strengthening southerly flows. Historical March data shows Warsaw averages 9-11°C highs, but this anomalously warm spring—fueled by reduced Arctic air incursions—elevates odds for 14°C (21.5%) and 16°C (12.5%), while extremes remain unlikely barring model busts common in transitional seasons. Traders eye tomorrow's 12Z updates for shifts.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Warsaw Chopin Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 23 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Warsaw Chopin Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/pl/warsaw/EPWA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Consensus among leading ensemble weather models like ECMWF and GFS drives the 51.5% implied probability for Warsaw's highest temperature hitting 15°C on March 23, with recent runs converging on mild highs of 14-16°C amid a high-pressure ridge ushering Atlantic warmth into Central Europe. Official Polish IMGW forecasts align, projecting daytime peaks near 15°C under partly cloudy skies, up from cooler ensemble means a week ago due to strengthening southerly flows. Historical March data shows Warsaw averages 9-11°C highs, but this anomalously warm spring—fueled by reduced Arctic air incursions—elevates odds for 14°C (21.5%) and 16°C (12.5%), while extremes remain unlikely barring model busts common in transitional seasons. Traders eye tomorrow's 12Z updates for shifts.
